perturbation.original_text: Subcontractor warrants to EDGE that the Work will be performed with promptness and diligence and shall be executed in a quality manner consistent with workmanship standards in the particular trades involved.
perturbation.changed_text: Subcontractor warrants to EDGE that the Work will be performed with reasonable effort, considering other projects, and shall be executed in a quality manner that meets the minimum requirements of the trades involved.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by weakening the warranty standard for the Work. Originally, the Subcontractor warranted 'promptness and diligence' and 'quality manner consistent with workmanship standards'. The change to 'reasonable effort, considering other projects' and 'minimum requirements' lowers the bar, potentially conflicting with the original, higher standard. This creates uncertainty about the expected level of quality and effort required from the Subcontractor. Particularly creates a contradiction to section 30B: '...performance by Subcontractor shall be required to the extent consistent with the Contract Documents to produce the intended results.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The documents referenced in this Agreement are complementary, and what is called for by any one shall be as binding as if called for by all. The intention of the Agreements, Exhibits, and Purchase Order (collectively "Contract Documents") is to include all labor, materials, supervision, equipment, transportation and expense necessary for the proper execution of the Work; performance by Subcontractor shall be required to the extent consistent with the Contract Documents to produce the intended results. If a conflict or inconsistency exists between the provisions of this Agreement, the pre-printed terms and conditions on either side of the Purchase Order, any other document, the order of precedence to resolve the conflict or inconsistency is as follows: (a) the Agreement; (b) Exhibits to the Agreement; (c) Purchase Orders; and (d) drawings and specifications. In the event of an irreconcilable conflict, discrepancy, error, or omission, the provision imposing the greater duty on Subcontractor shall apply. Materials or work described in words that have a well-known technical or trade meaning shall be held to refer to such recognized standards.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the original warranty by changing 'promptness and diligence' to 'reasonable effort, considering other projects' and 'quality manner consistent with workmanship standards' to 'minimum requirements of the trades involved.' This creates a contradiction to the contradicted text which states '...performance by Subcontractor shall be required to the extent consistent with the Contract Documents to produce the intended results.' The modified text now allows for a lower standard of performance which directly conflicts with the original intention that the subcontractor's performance should produce the intended results to the extent consistent with the contract documents, which originally included a higher standard of workmanship.

perturbation.original_text: After fifteen (15) days prior written notice to Subcontractor to cure, or additional time as may be reasonably required by EDGE to cure the event of default, EDGE may terminate this Agreement, or any one or more awarded Purchase Orders and take control of the Work for such terminated Purchase Orders, including any or all materials for the Work, and may proceed with the completion of the Work as contemplated by this Agreement by whatever method deemed expedient by EDGE upon the occurrence of any of the following events, which shall be deemed events of a default by Subcontractor hereunder:
perturbation.changed_text: After fifteen (15) days prior written notice to Subcontractor to cure, EDGE may terminate this Agreement, or any one or more awarded Purchase Orders and take control of the Work for such terminated Purchase Orders, including any or all materials for the Work, and may proceed with the completion of the Work as contemplated by this Agreement by whatever method deemed expedient by EDGE upon the occurrence of any of the following events, which shall be deemed events of a default by Subcontractor hereunder:
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or additional time as may be reasonably required by EDGE to cure the event of default' this creates an in-text contradiction. The original text allows for additional time to cure beyond the 15 days, which is a conditional statement of 15 days or an added reasonable time for the cure, which the perturbation removes. In Section 11.A it list the 'events of default' that will trigger the termination. The added time to cure can conflict with events that if not cured in additional time will be detrimental to both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1) Subcontractor fails to supply a sufficient number of skilled workers or suitable materials or equipment for performance of the Work;
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause 'or additional time as may be reasonably required by EDGE to cure the event of default'. The original text allowed for an extension beyond the initial 15 days if EDGE deemed it reasonable. The removal of this clause directly contradicts the situation described in the 'Contradicted Text', which is 'Subcontractor fails to supply a sufficient number of skilled workers or suitable materials or equipment for performance of the Work'. This failure might necessitate more than 15 days to rectify. By eliminating the allowance for reasonable additional time, the changed text now creates an immediate event of default after 15 days, irrespective of the circumstances, directly conflicting with the potential need for more time as implied by the nature of the 'Contradicted Text' event. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: EDGE shall have the opportunity to review and inspect all elements of the Work in a reasonable manner. EDGE shall have the right to require repair or replacement of any Work which is defective or not performed in accordance with the Purchase Order or deviates from other requirements of this Agreement, provided Subcontractor shall have until acceptance to complete such repair or replacement. Subcontractor shall be solely responsible for all construction means, methods, techniques, procedures and safety and security programs in connection with the performance of the Work.
perturbation.changed_text: EDGE shall have the opportunity to review and inspect all elements of the Work in a reasonable manner, but such inspection is not required. EDGE shall have the right to require repair or replacement of any Work which is defective or not performed in accordance with the Purchase Order or deviates from other requirements of this Agreement, however, all such defects must be reported within 72 hours of discovery. Subcontractor shall be solely responsible for all construction means, methods, techniques, procedures and safety and security programs in connection with the performance of the Work.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the timeframe for reporting defects and making inspections optional. Originally, EDGE was responsible to review and inspect all elements of the work as well. This change introduces that inspection isn't 'required'. Further, the addition of 'all such defects must be reported within 72 hours of discovery' directly contradicts section 17.C where EDGE has a year for reporting the defects. The combination of 72 hours reporting period and not requiring inspection, can cause harm to EDGE when trying to report defects discovered later on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If within one (1) year from the date of completion of Work acceptance, or within one (1) year from the completion of all other work or services and acceptance by EDGE (or within any longer materials warranty period as set forth above), any defects exists or arise, then in each case upon receipt of notice of such defect, Subcontractor shall (unless EDGE chooses another remedy) promptly cause such defect(s) to be repaired or remedied at Subcontractor's sole cost and expense, including but not limited to the costs of transportation, uncovering, removal, disposal, replacement, correction, installation and covering. Subcontractor shall commence or cause the commencement of repairs immediately upon receipt of notice from EDGE and thereafter diligently pursue same to completion or cause the same to be diligently pursued to completion. EDGE shall have the right without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to it, (i) to make such repairs and offset the cost thereof against any amounts owed to be paid by EDGE to Subcontractor or invoice Subcontractor therefore which invoice shall be paid net 30 days or (ii) require Subcontractor to refund the price of the Work not meeting the warranties. Subcontractor will extend the warranty period if Subcontractor has been grossly negligent in the performance of any Work under this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Subcontractor shall not be liable or responsible for any defect in any materials used by Subcontractor if such defect was not due to Subcontractor's acts or omissions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 72-hour reporting window for defects, which directly contradicts the original one-year window for reporting defects as stated in the contradicted text. This inconsistency regarding the timeframe for reporting defects creates a strong contradiction, making it unclear which timeframe should be followed, and thus affecting the enforceability of the agreement.
